title: brain watch — one NDJSON stream line (--json only)
description: Describes a SINGLE line of the `brain watch --json` NDJSON stream: a discriminated union over `event` across the 8 v1 event types (3 control: watch.hello / watch.heartbeat / watch.error; 5 domain: job / model_call / audit / backup / daemon). Every event line carries v:1, event, at (RFC-3339 ms UTC). The one non-event line the stream may emit is the standard single error envelope (error-envelope.schema.json) — sole line on startup failure, or final line after a mid-stream fatal fault. Consumers MUST ignore unknown `event` values (additive event types do not bump v; spec §15). All payloads are allowlisted metadata only — never note content, job payloads, or model text. The `audit` member's eventType enum is a replica of the audit_events DDL CHECK (the SSOT); tools/watch-eventtype-drift.test.ts pins it. The `job` member's payload replicates @atlas/jobs JobListRow (the SSOT shape); tools/watch-job-shape-drift.test.ts pins it.

v.description: Stream-contract version; bumped only on a breaking envelope/taxonomy change.

at.description: RFC-3339 ms UTC emission time (the emission clock, not a source timestamp).

auditHeadSeq.description: The safe checkpoint: the contiguous-committed-prefix high-water mark of the run.* seq space among rows already written to this stream (−1 = nothing yet; run.* seqs start at 0). Never the max emitted seq; db.*-space rows never enter it. During --since-seq replay the hello carries min(sinceSeq, prefix) — the pre-replay checkpoint (spec §7.4/§8.1).

path.description: The configured ledger DB path being watched (attached or not).

snapshot.description: The status --json shape (same keys, same derivation code — spec §7.6) extended with daemon probes. While ledger.attached is false, only `daemons` is present — the ledger-derived keys are absent, never fabricated zeros.

openRuns.description: Count of non-terminal runs keyed by workflow state.

head.description: The refs/audit/runs head commit, or empty when no events exist.

anchorSource.description: "git" when verified via the broker; "sqlite-only" when the broker was unreachable — a DEGRADED verdict, the protected ref itself is unverified.

resume.description: Absent (never null) while ledger.attached is false — no fabricated cursor.

replay.description: Present iff --since-seq (and the ledger is attached); absent otherwise — never null. `events` counts the run.*-space rows re-sent as ordinary audit lines immediately after this hello.

resume.description: The cursor checkpoint (k8s-BOOKMARK dual). Absent while ledger.attached is false — a detached heartbeat carries no cursor.

code.description: Stable machine classification of the fault.

message.description: Free-text detail. C0/C1 control characters MUST be escaped (JSON string escaping); one of the two free-text fields in the stream (with job.lastError).

job.description: Current-state sample of a changed job row — the jobs-list item shape (@atlas/jobs JobListRow, the SSOT projection; spec §7.6). May coalesce: one event carries the latest state seen at a poll tick, not every transition.

nextRunAt.description: RFC-3339 next-eligible time (backoff); absent for terminal jobs.

lastError.description: Stable classification of the last failure (allowlisted metadata); absent when none. C0/C1 control characters MUST be escaped; one of the two free-text fields in the stream (with watch.error.message).

updatedAt.description: RFC-3339 UTC time of the last job-row mutation.

modelCall.description: A new model_calls row (insert-only) — the DDL columns, camelCased. Exactly once per ledger incarnation; no replay.

audit.description: A new audit_events row, from either seq space: run.* rows (gapless from 0) and non-run.% ledger-internal rows (db.* + evidence.retry_enqueued, allocated from DB_EVENT_SEQ_BASE = 10^12). Both stream live; the resume cursor and --since-seq replay cover the run.* space only. payload_hash is deliberately omitted (an integrity internal).

eventType.description: Replica of the audit_events DDL CHECK allowlist (the SSOT — sqlite-store migrations/0001_core.ts); tools/watch-eventtype-drift.test.ts fails CI when a DDL kind is added without this enum.

gitHead.description: refs/audit/runs head returned by the broker append; absent when the DDL column is NULL.

backup.description: The backup_watermark single row after any column change — mirroring the status --json backup object naming: the DDL column seq is exposed as watermarkSeq so event and snapshot correlate on one name. May coalesce to the latest state seen at a tick.

lastBackupAt.description: RFC-3339; absent when no backup has completed yet.

daemon.description: A daemon reachability TRANSITION (probed at heartbeat cadence + once at start) — never emitted for an unchanged state. previousReachable lets a consumer render the transition without state.

x-atlas-contract.summary: Long-lived NDJSON stream of jobs, model calls, ledger appends, backup watermark, daemon health.

flags.name: --since-seq <n>
flags.description: Replay durable run.*-space audit rows with seq > n (exclusive) before going live; -1 replays from row 0.

flags.name: --once
flags.description: Emit watch.hello (the full snapshot) and exit 0.

notes: --json is REQUIRED: watch is machine-only; invocation without --json is a usage error (exit 5).

Output is an NDJSON stream — one JSON object per line, each line validating against this schema's oneOf; the single error envelope (sole line on startup failure, final line on mid-stream fatal fault) is the one non-event line.
Consumers MUST ignore unknown event values (additive taxonomy growth does not bump v).
prohibitedEffects: no vault, projection, or ledger write of any kind — the ledger is opened readonly:true (can never create, migrate, checkpoint, or write)
no Atlas lock taken in any scope
no git repository opened; no git ref read or written
no run.readonly (or any) audit event emitted — watch is a plain read, not an audited read
no network egress; no provider call; no egress-broker call
no broker method called other than the read-only getAuditChainStatus (snapshot only, best-effort)

errorCodes.when: missing --json, unknown flag, out-of-range value, or --once combined with --since-seq
the configured vault/repo is missing or unreadable
unexpected internal fault; also a broker PROTOCOL error during the snapshot probe (never mere unreachability, which is data)

examples.message: ledger file replaced at path (device/inode changed); re-attaching
